title: Career Journey Progression - Factual Timeline Source
content:
Career progression summary:
- Foundation stage (2019): Software Engineering Intern at Infosys, where early full-stack work and analytics exposure led to a shift toward data-focused problem solving.
- ML transition stage (2020-2023): Machine Learning Engineer at Cognizant, focused on production NLP and document intelligence across enterprise domains.
- Academic deepening stage (2023-2025): M.S. in Data Science at UMBC, adding formal training in ML, NLP, deep learning, cloud systems, and statistical learning.
- Innovation stage (2025): AI/ML programming internship at R/SEEK (UMBC), focused on edge AI and real-time embedded computer vision.
- GenAI specialization stage (2025-present): Data Scientist in GenAI and ML at Enigma Technologies, focused on production RAG pipelines, LLM fine-tuning, and explainability.

Education and capability growth:
- M.S. Data Science at UMBC (2023-2025), with capstone work in RAG systems, financial sentiment analysis, and applied computer vision.
- Progression from software engineering fundamentals to production ML systems and then to GenAI platform delivery.

Cross-stage outcomes repeatedly emphasized in the source guides:
- Operational efficiency improvements in document intelligence workflows.
- Production deployment discipline including validation, monitoring, and reliability.
- Consistent movement from implementation roles to architecture and leadership-oriented ownership.
